gaps.statement: Validation does not yet reject overlapping Module paths or tracked files owned by no Module.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before automatic worker write capabilities are enabled.

gaps.statement: Minimum Gate commands are selected by primary Module, but are not yet pruned further by exact Verification Obligations or Contract deltas.
gaps.expansionTrigger: When a Module's focused command stops being the smallest high-confidence route for routine Changes.

gaps.statement: Exit streams, signals, and byte truncation are characterized locally; timeout abort, encoding boundaries, and cross-platform subprocess behavior are not.
The browser projection has no DOM-level test proving that it preserves Project Model and Change semantics.

The Development Plan and Change alignment are machine-readable, but no bounded, snapshot-consistent Project Atlas profile yet projects Outcome to Claim, Gate, current or historical Evidence, Residual Uncertainty, and Knowledge Gap from their existing fact authorities.

The Change Store supplies stable bounded zero-write Profile snapshots, and Project Model now supplies an accepted opaque exact-Model-bound Claim Gate route product with immutable operational observations and a pure in-memory Profile reuse seam. The remaining projection boundaries are not closed: Change Kernel has declared but not implemented one all-Contract-Claim current-Model product, one query-demand union product per unique verified frozen Governance Baseline, and one aggregate query budget; no Kernel-owned Workbench view model supplies selectable Claims, exact routes, and disabled reasons; and browser compatibility code can still express aggregate score, confidence, percentage, health, readiness, or green-light semantics.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Implement the governed Change Kernel route-product consumption and aggregate budget before the Workbench slice; then compile the Workbench view model and remove aggregate assurance compatibility before browser projection or LGT-010 closure.

gaps.statement: Accepted Change history lives only in Git-ignored local runtime state, so a fresh clone or another workspace cannot reproduce the historical Evidence portion of an Architecture Acceptance Profile from versioned repository content alone.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before Architecture Acceptance Profiles are exchanged across clones or workspaces, used for remote review, or relied on as durable audit artifacts.

gaps.statement: Ordinary Changes now bind exact Claims visible through the primary Module's owned or dependency Contracts to stable active Outcome Criteria; unresolved or ambiguous matches fail closed, and explicit non-progress exceptions require frozen Development Plan authority.
gaps.resolution: Enforced compilation derives content-addressed Outcome Contributions, and the Kernel re-derives their acceptance-bound projection from the frozen Governance Baseline; exceptions grant no progress and cannot prove transitions. Contributions prove relevance, not Criterion satisfaction.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Exact Contribution derivation, enforced rejection, Plan-authority routing, or acceptance-time rederivation is weakened.

gaps.statement: Development Outcome activation, achievement, and conditional activation now compile from a Candidate-frozen catalog of prior sealed Accepted Packages, exact Contribution and Gate Evidence bindings, closed Gap dispositions, and explicit Plan-authority Criterion assessments.
gaps.resolution: The Project Model owns deterministic route and proof sufficiency semantics; the Kernel freezes and content-addresses the prior Package catalog, resolves Store records, binds the compiler projection into the Verification Subject and Accepted Package, and re-runs resolution before Gates and acceptance. Declared mode remains observable for adopters, while this repository enforces missing or incomplete Transitions.
gaps.reopenTrigger: The Candidate catalog can be regenerated after creation; historical Package seals, exact Contribution or Gate Evidence bindings, Gap dispositions, authority assessments, Gate-time re-resolution, or acceptance-time re-resolution are weakened.

gaps.statement: Workers have no capability sandbox and authority identities are local unauthenticated assertions.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before remote workers, multi-user acceptance, or signed attestations.

gaps.statement: Legatura self-hosts its Project Model, but no real brownfield app, apk, and relay repository proves incremental adoption without whole-repository modeling.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before Legatura is presented as a reusable framework for an existing software repository.

gaps.statement: The Kernel permanently freezes every declared Outcome statement, so speculative planned or conditional meanings cannot yet be revised through append-only history before activation.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before a planned or conditional Outcome needs semantic refinement or any S2 Outcome is activated.
